macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indexes Decline on February 27
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.45% to 6,878, Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.92% to 22,668, and Dow Jones lost 1.1% or 521 points to 48,978 amid worries over AI, inflation, and geopolitical tensions. Oil prices rose 2.8% to $67.02 per barrel due to US-Iran concerns.[1][3]

items.title: Coupang Shares Rise Despite Q4 Earnings Hit by Settlement
items.summary: Coupang closed up 2% after Q4 earnings impacted by a $1.18 billion settlement over data breach, while peers JD.com and Alibaba fell. Investors focused on stabilization signs.[1]

items.title: Netflix Surges After Dropping Warner Bros. Bid
items.summary: Netflix climbed 13.8% after abandoning its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming, boosting Paramount Skydance shares 20.8% while Warner fell 2.2%.[3]

items.title: Inflation Data Raises Doubts on Fed Rate Cuts
items.summary: A discouraging inflation update pressured markets and could delay Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, with 10-year Treasury yield falling to 3.96% amid investor caution.[3]

items.title: Canadian TSX Hits Record Highs Driven by Materials and Banks
items.summary: S&P/TSX Composite closed at record 34,502 up 1.1%, fueled by materials sector gains and strong bank earnings amid Bank of Canada steady policy rate.[4]

items.title: US Indexes Show Technical Rebound Potential Amid Volatility
items.summary: Dow Jones dipped to near 48,600 support before rebounding, S&P 500 mixed near 6,890 MAs, Nasdaq eyes 25,000 resistance with month-end flows expected.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Drops 2.70% to $65,592.90
items.summary: The CoinDesk Bitcoin Price Index fell 2.70% or $1,821.18 to $65,592.90 on February 27, marking two consecutive down days and the worst weekly performance since early February. It traded as low as $65,132.73 amid broader year-to-date declines of 25.08%.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $66,000 USDT on Binance
items.summary: Bitcoin crossed the 66,000 USDT mark, trading at 66,005.68 USDT as of February 28, 00:14 UTC, signaling a brief recovery after recent declines.[3]

items.title: Over $266 Million in Bitcoin Withdrawn from Leading Exchange
items.summary: Whale Alert reported large Bitcoin withdrawals totaling over $266 million from a leading crypto exchange on February 28, indicating significant whale activity.[5]

items.title: Binance Partners with Africell for Crypto Education in Africa
items.summary: Binance and telecom operator Africell announced a collaboration to expand blockchain education and crypto access across African markets using mobile networks.[1]

items.title: Ripple CEO Hints at Elon Musk Partnership for XRP Ledger
items.summary: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse suggested a potential partnership with Elon Musk to boost visibility and liquidity on the XRP Ledger, amid launches like REAL Token on BTCC.[1]

items.title: Coinbase Expands into Tokenized Equities with Partnership
items.summary: Coinbase partnered to advance tokenized equities and stock trading services under SEC oversight, building on its equity trading pilot for integrated financial platforms.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokémon Presents Reveals Next-Gen Games Pokémon Winds and Waves for Switch 2 in 2027
items.summary: The Pokémon Company announced Pokémon Winds and Pokémon Waves, new mainline titles set in a tropical open-world region, launching globally in 2027 exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 during the February 27 Pokémon Day 30th anniversary stream.[1][2][5]

items.title: Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen Remakes Launch on Nintendo Switch Today
items.summary: The Game Boy Advance remakes of the original Pokémon games, Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen, released immediately on Nintendo Switch and Switch 2 following the Pokémon Presents, featuring Pokémon Home integration.[1][2]

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Life Sim Spinoff Set for March 5 Release on Switch 2
items.summary: Pokémon Pokopia, a new life simulation game with cooking and multiplayer features, is scheduled to launch on Nintendo Switch 2 on March 5, 2026, as revealed in the Pokémon Presents trailer.[2]

items.title: Pokémon Champions Battle Game Arrives April 2026 on Switch and Mobile
items.summary: Pokémon Champions, a competitive battle-focused title with cross-platform play and Pokémon Home support, will release on Nintendo Switch in April 2026, with a mobile version later this year.[1][2]

items.title: Pokémon World Championships 2026 Announced for San Francisco
items.summary: The Pokémon World Championships 2026 will be held alongside Pokémon XP in San Francisco, as revealed by Global Esports & Events head Chris Brown during the February 2026 Pokémon Presents.[4]

items.title: Tales of Berseria Remastered Launches Today
items.summary: Bandai Namco announced and launched Tales of Berseria Remastered, an enhanced version of the action RPG, on February 28, 2026.[3]

items.title: Pokémon Game Music Collection and Trading Card Set Teased for 30th Anniversary
items.summary: A jukebox with 45 songs from Pokémon Red and Blue released today, and a new 30th anniversary Pokémon Trading Card Game set is coming in 2026, highlighted in the Pokémon Presents.[1]

items.title: Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness Joins Nintendo Switch Online in March
items.summary: The GameCube spin-off Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness is coming to Nintendo Switch Online's GameCube library in March 2026.[1]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google Launches Nano Banana 2 with Improved Speed and 4K Capabilities
items.summary: Google has released Nano Banana 2, featuring enhanced speed and 4K image creation capabilities. This update addresses performance improvements in a competitive market.

items.title: Tim Cook Hints at Major Apple Product Launches Next Week
items.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook has teased a 'big week' ahead, with the company preparing to unveil a series of new products starting March 2. This signals significant upcoming hardware announcements.

items.title: Samsung Unveils Latest Phones with Key Features for Everyday Users
items.summary: Tech journalist Lexy Savvides discusses Samsung's newest smartphones, highlighting practical upgrades that matter most for daily use. The episode aired on February 28, 2026.

items.title: Brink’s to Acquire NCR Atleos for $6.6 Billion
items.summary: Security firm Brink’s announced a $6.6 billion acquisition of NCR Atleos, a major move in the fintech and ATM sectors. This deal was highlighted in top fintech news for the week ending February 27.

items.title: US Fintech Block Cuts Over 4,000 Jobs
items.summary: Block, the company behind Square, is slashing more than 4,000 positions amid workforce reductions. This was one of the top fintech stories for the week.

items.title: Crypto.com Receives Conditional Approval for US National Trust Bank Charter
items.summary: Crypto.com has gained conditional approval for a US national trust bank charter, marking a key regulatory milestone. This development was noted in recent fintech updates.

items.title: Citi Sells Additional 24% Stake in Banamex for $2.5 Billion
items.summary: Citigroup sold a further 24% stake in its Mexican unit Banamex for $2.5 billion, advancing its divestiture plans. This financial tech deal topped weekly news.
